ip_outcome_note: Confirmed Impidimp via PokemonTCG.io API card swsh2-123; National Pokedex no. 859, Dark/Fairy-type imp Pokemon, introduced in Generation VIII (Pokemon Sword & Shield).

trivia_bullets.text: Impidimp (National Pokedex no. 859) and its evolution line (Morgrem, Grimmsnarl) are the only Pokemon with the Dark/Fairy type combination — no other Pokemon in the franchise shares it.
sources: WebSearch: CyberPost 'Is there a Dark Fairy type Pokemon?'

trivia_bullets.text: Impidimp's design draws from European folklore mischievous fairy-like creatures: imps, goblins, gremlins, and boggarts. One specific possible reference is the Lincoln Imp, a grotesque stone-carved figure in Lincoln Cathedral, England — fitting for the UK-modeled Galar region. Impidimp and Morgrem are the only pink Dark-type Pokemon.

"Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy." The negative-energy-feeding mechanic is the design spine of the entire line.

Grimmsnarl (final evolution) became a competitive staple in Sword & Shield doubles formats due to unique access to both Prankster and powerful support moves (Reflect, Light Screen, Thunder Wave) — uncommon on a Dark-type.

related_cards.name: Hatenna
related_cards.note: Co-introduced Fairy-side SwSh species; shares set and design-wave context
